introSection: Thunderhawk is a wooden roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was constructed by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Originally called The Coaster, the ride's formal opening was held on May 4, 1924 though the roller coaster had operated on five previous Sundays.

In 1930, the layout was changed after the first drop.

The ride was repainted yellow in 1954.

The Coaster was renamed Thunderhawk for the 1989 season following a renovation by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company, who also built the trains for Dorney Park's new wooden roller coaster Hercules.

The ride received a new loading platform in 1995.

The ride was refurbished with new trains in 2016.

On June 23, 2021, the ride was designated a Roller Coaster Landmark by American Coaster Enthusiasts during Coaster Con 43.

To commemorate the ride's 100th Anniversary in 2024, the ride received refurbished trains from Philadelphia Toboggan Company, special decals, new signs, new merchandise, and a special event to celebrate the milestone.

fullText:
Thunderhawk is a wooden roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was constructed by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

Originally called The Coaster, the ride's formal opening was held on May 4, 1924 though the roller coaster had operated on five previous Sundays.

In 1930, the layout was changed after the first drop.

The ride was repainted yellow in 1954.

The Coaster was renamed Thunderhawk for the 1989 season following a renovation by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company, who also built the trains for Dorney Park's new wooden roller coaster Hercules.

The ride received a new loading platform in 1995.

The ride was refurbished with new trains in 2016.

On June 23, 2021, the ride was designated a Roller Coaster Landmark by American Coaster Enthusiasts during Coaster Con 43.

To commemorate the ride's 100th Anniversary in 2024, the ride received refurbished trains from Philadelphia Toboggan Company, special decals, new signs, new merchandise, and a special event to celebrate the milestone.
